minor.note: Locked: the Jan 2015 low is essentially undisputed historical fact (Mt. Gox collapse, see module docstring for independently confirming sources), not subject to promotion. Unlike ^GSPC's GSC (III), no (III)-equivalent peak label is offered here for the real 2013 top ($1,135) -- that peak predates BTC-USD.json's own 2014-09-17 start, so there is nothing in this repo's own fetched data to verify it against, the same gap class as ^DJI's own pre-1971 Norris citation.

labels.note: start of (V) = the (IV) trough, same locked convention as GLD's/SLV's own Minor-tier-up Intermediate (V) (dashboard_build_spec.md §27), one degree lower

primary_label.description: Project-derived hypothesis: a single Minute-degree advance in progress since the 2015-01-14 Minor (IV) trough, per the Minor (V) framing above. NOT subdivided into Minute i-v internal waves here -- same reasoning as GLD's/SLV's own primary_label: the only pivot detector available ('slow' scale, Intermediate/40wk-18mo degree) does not resolve finer-than-Minor structure distinctly, so asserting specific Minute i-v turn dates from it would overstate precision. Left for human review on the deep-history panel. This advance includes BTC's real all-time high (126,198.07 on 2025-10-06) and a real, still-recent ~50% pullback since (to the low-$60k area as of this bootstrap) -- a genuine, large intra-advance correction, not a reversal: the 2015 invalidation level below remains unbroken, and every prior correction of comparable severity (2018, 2022) was followed by a new all-time high, not by this structure's own invalidation.

alternates.label: Multiple financial-history sources (Forex.com/Bitget retrospectives): Bitcoin fell from $1,135 (Dec 4, 2013) to $175 (Jan 14, 2015), an 85% peak-to-trough decline driven by the collapse of Mt. Gox (then the world's largest Bitcoin exchange, withdrawals halted Feb 2014)
alternates.caveat: Factual/historical price retrospective, not an Elliott Wave source -- confirms the real-world significance and near-exact date/level of this bootstrap's own fixed low, not a wave-labeling claim.

alternates.label: General Elliott Wave crypto-analysis commentary: Bitcoin's periodic corrections between 2012-2018 repeatedly completed a wave (a)-(b)-(c) decline coinciding with the 200-week EMA, with 2015 and 2018 both recognized as cycle-completion lows; halving events are independently described as aligning with the START of major Wave 3 impulses at the highest degree
alternates.caveat: General crypto-EW commentary aggregated across multiple sources in the search results, not one named practitioner's specific count of THIS exact low -- treated as background corroboration that 2015 is a recognized cycle-low candidate in the EW crypto community, not ground truth for this bootstrap's own degree choice (Minor, per module docstring's own independent 'shorter window' reasoning).

invalidation.basis: A confirmed daily close below the real 2015-01-14 low would invalidate the Minor (V)/Minute-in-progress hypothesis.

history_note: BTC-USD.json covers 2014-09-17 to 2026-07-06 (4310 rows) -- real intraday OHLC from its very first row. Own-longest-series self-anchor (dashboard_build_spec.md §2), independent of any other instrument's degree claim -- Bitcoin is its own asset class, no equity/bond/metal/oil 'principle of commonality' stand-in applies. The fixed Minor (IV)/(V) anchor intentionally omits a (III)-equivalent peak label for the real 2013 top ($1,135, per the module docstring's cited sources) -- that peak predates this repo's own fetched history.
